answer.recommended_action: Audit and recalibrate the detector bias supply: a 56% dark-count surge with only +0.026 °C thermal change (ev.detector_health_01, bias_drift=true) indicates a bias-voltage or gain-register shift rather than a thermal event. Measure actual APD reverse-bias voltage against nominal; adjust or replace the bias regulator if out-of-spec. If bias supply is confirmed stable, the APD itself has drifted — schedule detector module replacement. Confirm dark-count rate returns to baseline and effective range recovers to ≥120 m before closing H5. H1 (window contamination) remains open and should be cleared concurrently with a visual/optical-power-throughput check, but the detector-localised evidence makes it a secondary check rather than the primary action.

hypotheses.label: Window contamination — partial obscuration of optics reducing transmitted/received signal power
Calibration drift — range-scale or offset error introduced by firmware/config change or parameter decay
Laser aging — gradual output power decline reducing SNR at distance
Thermal/TEC fault — elevated or uncontrolled junction temperature degrading laser efficiency and/or detector sensitivity
Detector drift — responsivity or dark-current shift reducing effective sensitivity at threshold

evidence.summary: Laser output 47.9 mW vs baseline 48.0 mW (−0.34%); flagged non-low-power. Change is negligible relative to the ~8% range degradation under investigation. Laser source is at-spec.

config_diff returned no relevant changes (any_change=false, changed=[]). No firmware update, range-scale edit, or offset parameter modification is present in the configuration history. This eliminates the config/firmware-change sub-cause of calibration drift; parameter decay remains theoretically open but the primary mechanism for H2 is now unsupported.

Dark-count rate rose +56% while detector temperature increased only +0.026 °C with a confirmed live sensor (detector_temp_live=true); bias_drift=true. A 56% dark-count surge with negligible thermal change indicates non-thermal detector bias/gain drift. Elevated dark current raises the noise floor, compressing the SNR margin at distance and is fully consistent with the observed ~8% effective-range reduction. The result explicitly excludes thermal elevation as the mechanism, and the detector-specific localisation argues against laser, optical, or upstream-supply causes.

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

No evidence has been collected yet and all hypotheses are at equal prior. A laser_power_check is the single most discriminating first step: (a) if measured output power is nominal it strongly argues *against* H3 (laser aging) and the laser-side branch of H4 (TEC/thermal → laser efficiency loss) and hyp.upstream_power, collapsing the differential toward H2/H5/H1; (b) if power is measurably degraded (~15% drop would explain ~8% range loss assuming range ∝ √P) it elevates H3 and H4 and triggers the thermal/TEC path next. This single result cleaves the hypothesis space in half — source-side causes vs. receiver/calibration-side causes — without redundantly re-examining anything already known.

Three hypotheses are equally open (H1 window contamination, H2 calibration drift, H5 detector drift — all at log_odds 0.00). Laser source is confirmed at-spec (ev.laser_power_01), so optical-source explanations are weakened. The most efficient single discriminator now is config_diff: a calibration-parameter or firmware change is the only candidate that would produce a uniform ~8% range-scale or offset error without touching laser power or introducing spatial intensity variation. If a parameter change is found whose timestamp aligns with degradation onset (~1 week ago), H2 rises sharply and H1/H5 fall; if the config is clean, H2 is largely eliminated and the contest narrows to H1 vs H5, driving the next action to spatial_intensity_check or detector_health_check. No prior evidence speaks to config state, so this check adds maximum new information.

H1 (window contamination) and H5 (detector drift) are the only two open hypotheses, both at log_odds=0.00. Laser output is at-spec (ev.laser_power_01) and config is unchanged (ev.config_diff_01), so the degradation must originate either in the optical path (H1) or the detector subsystem (H5). detector_health_check interrogates responsivity and dark-current directly — independent of the transmit optical path — giving a clean internal/external split: a nominal result rules out H5 and strongly implicates H1, while an anomalous result (elevated dark current or reduced responsivity consistent with ~8% SNR loss) confirms H5 and argues against H1. No existing evidence has touched the detector, so this is the highest-information-gain action available.
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
